message:
feat(simd_scan): add stateful cross-chunk SIMD scanner for ANSI/UTF-8 boundary handling

Implement carry-state scanning so that ANSI escape sequences and
multi-byte UTF-8 characters that span chunk boundaries are correctly
handled in streaming ingest rather than producing spurious newline or
escape counts at split points.

Core API (simd_scan.rs):
- New OutputScanState struct carrying in_escape bool and
  pending_utf8_continuations count across chunk boundaries
- New public scan_newlines_and_ansi_with_state() dispatcher that
  selects memchr or scalar backend based on feature flags
- scan_newlines_and_ansi_memchr_with_state(): memchr3-based fast path
  that tracks escape entry/exit and UTF-8 continuation bytes across
  invocations
- scan_newlines_and_ansi_scalar_with_state(): byte-by-byte fallback
  with identical state tracking semantics
- update_utf8_pending() helper for UTF-8 continuation byte tracking
  with error recovery (resets pending count on invalid sequences)
- 2 new unit tests: escape sequence spanning chunk boundary (ESC[
  split across two chunks), partial UTF-8 4-byte emoji split across
  chunks with correct continuation tracking

Benchmarks (benches/simd_scan.rs):
- New benchmark group simd_scan_chunked_stateful comparing fast_stateful
  vs scalar_stateful across chunk sizes 16/32/64/128/256 on a 2MB
  ANSI-heavy synthetic payload
- ScalarState reference implementation and
  scalar_scan_reference_with_state() baseline for apples-to-apples
  comparison

Property tests (tests/proptest_simd_scan.rs):
- streaming_scan_matches_full_scan_on_arbitrary_chunks: arbitrary bytes
  split at random points, verifies stitched streaming metrics equal
  full-scan metrics (newline count, escape count, max line length)
- utf8_boundary_splits_preserve_metrics: valid UTF-8 text split at
  arbitrary byte offsets, verifies metric fidelity and no leftover
  partial UTF-8 state after final chunk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
